slides.tagline: [One-line manifesto — e.g. 'We make brands impossible to ignore.']
slides.notes: Cover slide. The studio name and tagline should dominate. No filler text. If you have a signature visual mark or logo, use it. Everything on this slide should feel intentional and confident.

slides.headline: [Your brand manifesto — one provocative, memorable line that captures how you think about design and work. e.g. 'Good work takes as long as it takes. Great work takes slightly longer.']
slides.subtext: [Optional: 2–3 sentences expanding on the manifesto. Only include if they add to the impact, not just explain it.]
slides.notes: Manifesto slide is about values, not services. It should make the right clients say 'yes, that's us' and the wrong ones self-select out. Be specific enough to be interesting.

slides.title: [Studio Name] is a [type] studio based in [City].
slides.body: [2–3 sentences: who you are, what you do, and who you do it for. The 'About' section should feel human — avoid agency clichés like 'passionate team of creatives.']
slides.notes: About slide should be short. The work speaks louder. Mention 4–6 capabilities max — focus on what you do best, not everything you could possibly do. Location and founding year add legitimacy.

slides.project_type: [Project Type — e.g. Brand Refresh / Campaign / Product Launch]
slides.headline: [Project Name or Campaign Line]
slides.outcome: [Key result in one line — e.g. '+140% brand recall in target demographic after launch']
slides.visual_hint: [Describe the hero visual: mockups, campaign imagery, before/after, etc.]
slides.notes: First case study: lead with your best work. Show the transformation, not just the deliverables. What was broken before? What did you change? What was the measurable impact? If you can show before/after, do it.

slides.headline: [Project Name or Campaign Line]
slides.outcome: [Key result — different metric than Case Study 01 to show range]
slides.notes: Second case study: pick a different industry or challenge to show versatility. Don't repeat the same type of project. If Case Study 01 was brand identity, show digital product or campaign here.

slides.title: [Number that defines your scale or impact]
slides.notes: Numbers slide: choose stats that build confidence, not vanity metrics. 'Projects delivered' is more credible than 'years of passion.' If you have press features or award logos, this is where to show them.

slides.title: How we work

steps.label: Discovery
steps.desc: [What happens in this phase — e.g. 'Stakeholder interviews, brand audit, competitive landscape mapping. We listen before we draw.']

steps.label: Strategy
steps.desc: [e.g. 'Positioning workshop, messaging architecture, creative direction. We align on what we're building before we build it.']

steps.label: Design & Build
steps.desc: [e.g. 'Iterative design sprints with weekly reviews. You see everything. Nothing ships without your sign-off.']

steps.label: Launch & Grow
steps.desc: [e.g. 'Rollout support, brand guidelines, and 90 days of post-launch monitoring. We don't disappear after delivery.']

slides.notes: Process slide reduces risk in the client's mind. It shows you're professional, predictable, and transparent. Avoid vague phase names — describe what actually happens in each.

slides.quote: [Best client quote — ideally describes the experience of working with you, not just the output. e.g. 'They challenged every assumption we had about our brand — and they were right every single time.']
slides.author: [Client Name]
slides.notes: One great testimonial beats a grid of 5-star ratings. The quote should address a potential client objection — are they reliable? Do they understand our industry? Will they push back when it matters?

slides.title: Your next project starts here.
slides.notes: Closing CTA: make the next step obvious and friction-free. If they're ready to talk, the email should be right here. Add a time-sensitive hook if relevant (e.g. 'We're accepting 2 new clients in Q3.').
